stories.headline: BlackRock's IBIT logs record $983M daily inflow as BTC rejects $80K wall
stories.tldr: IBIT pulled its biggest daily haul in six months even as bitcoin briefly tagged $79K and rolled back into range.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Spot-ETF demand keeps absorbing supply at the same level concentrated sellers keep defending — the longer that standoff lasts, the more violent the eventual break.
the_details: IBIT recorded $983M of net creations, its largest daily inflow since October 2025, per CoinDesk.
BTC printed a $79K high before sliding back, with analysts citing 'concentrated sell orders' between $80K-$85K.
Bitget Research's Ryan Lee sees BTC clearing $80K-$85K short-term on lower leverage and steady ETF bid.

stories.headline: Grayscale and Bitmine stake nearly $500M in ETH as spot ETFs flip to inflows
stories.tldr: Two of the largest US ETH allocators activated ~$500M of native staking while spot ETH ETFs took in $23.4M, snapping an outflow streak.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: When TradFi-aligned holders move from passive custody to validator yield, the float available to short ETH compresses — a quiet but durable supply shock.
the_details: Grayscale and Bitmine collectively staked ~$500M worth of ETH this week, per CoinMarketCap data.
US spot Ethereum ETFs logged $23.4M in net inflows, reversing a brief outflow streak.
Bitget Research targets ETH at $2,800-$3,000 short-term; spot trading near $2,320 after a softer open.

stories.headline: 100+ crypto firms led by Coinbase, Ripple press Senate to mark up CLARITY Act
stories.tldr: A coalition of more than 100 firms pushed the Senate Banking Committee to move the market-structure bill out of limbo and onto the floor.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The CLARITY Act is the fixed point industry strategy is built around — every delay extends the regulatory grey zone the SEC's interpretive guidance only patches.
the_details: Letter signed by Coinbase, Ripple and 100+ others delivered to Senate Banking April 23, per CoinDesk.
Bill would split spot crypto oversight between SEC and CFTC, codifying the March 17 joint taxonomy.
DL News flags May-July as the realistic markup window before the August recess kills 2026 timing.

stories.headline: TOKEN2049 Dubai pushed to 2027 as UAE security risks force conference reshuffle
stories.tldr: Organizers postponed the Dubai event by a full year, citing heightened regional security risks around the Strait of Hormuz tensions.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The flagship Middle East crypto conference moving on geopolitics drains a major liquidity event from the 2026 calendar and signals real fundraising risk in the region.
the_details: TOKEN2049 confirmed the Dubai 2026 edition is rescheduled to 2027, per The Block.
Organizers cited 'heightened security risks in the UAE' as Iran continues to seize ships in the Gulf.
Singapore edition still expected on schedule; sponsors pivoting allocations to APAC events.

stories.headline: Ripple CTO Schwartz flags sophisticated Robinhood phishing wave hitting users
stories.tldr: David Schwartz warned that attackers are abusing Robinhood's email infrastructure to send phishing lures that bypass standard spoofing checks.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Email-vector phishing on a regulated US broker is the cheapest, most scalable on-ramp for stealing self-custodied keys — expect copycats hitting Coinbase and Kraken users next.
the_details: Schwartz publicly flagged the campaign April 27 after receiving a phishing lure himself.
Lures appear to originate from Robinhood-owned domains, defeating naive SPF/DKIM checks.
Comes amid April's $606M hack toll, the worst month for crypto theft since February 2025.
